title: Backup/Export of Automations
excerpt:
is there anyway for us to “backup/export” our automations to excel or a plain text file. We have a very large amount of automations (over 300) and would really like to have them documented via an export process. 
Thanks

posts.author: Mondaymorningblues (mondaymorningblues)
posts.cookedHtml:
is there anyway for us to “backup/export” our automations to excel or a plain text file. We have a very large amount of automations (over 300) and would really like to have them documented via an export process.

Thanks

posts.author: Phill (Phill Allen)
posts.cookedHtml:
We currently have 14 boards, all of which all have Automations. One of our boards has 32 Automations!

It would be nice to be able to export to Excel, Word or some other format those Automation dialogs as a preview of the message rather than have to click on edit to see the contents. (see attached).
It can also be difficult to determine what has been selected for notifications when you see something like {pulse.status0} instead of Priority.

We rely heavily on automations to keep our team experiencing click burnout and this would go a long way to allowing refinement and updating of the automations we use.

Presentation11280×519 50.7 KB

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: HI @Phill! I love this idea, we are working with our team to implement a solution to add a note or label automations to give more detail into what each one is for - still working on how that will play out on the platform but it is a goal of ours within the next couple of iterations to start figuring out. Stay tuned for this!

posts.author: Phill (Phill Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! It would be greatly appreciated

posts.author: Christophe (SC)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I am in a very similar situation, less boards (6), but some of these boards have 53 automates.
It can easily happen that there is a conflict between some automates, and due to the way the automates are displayed, it is very tricky, if not almost impossible, to find and solve the conflict.
Alongside to this, a possibility to export the automates to Excel (or csv) would also be a great add-on when we need to implement the same automates across various boards (maybe an import system could be added in the same time).

Currently I try to keep an excel file up-to-date with the automates, but it is not really easy, moreover when multiple users (owners) can add new automates.

I share an example of the Excel I am maintaining (this one only includes 25 automates, but gives an idea

2021-07-21_14h42_192526×549 65.5 KB

).

posts.author: elmira.aliyeva (Elmira Aliyeva)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey! I have the same issue! Are there any updates?

posts.author: KamrynSmithAdcock (Kamryn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any updates to this feature? I have over 40 automations that I would need to see on a spreadsheet.

posts.author: Mondaymorningblues (mondaymorningblues)
posts.cookedHtml: We also have almost 400 automations covering 7 workflows on a board could really due with this functionality.

posts.author: Arsel (Acar)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Are there any updates on this? We would really love the ability to export our automations.

posts.author: mdalzell (Meredith Dalzell)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. Did this suggestion get implemented? I have hundreds of automations and integrations as well.

posts.author: tekMike (Mike hayles)
posts.cookedHtml:
I work with implementing CRM’s/MP systems into orgs and we have to document everything, for obvious reasons. How can something that is required at Mid-market and Enterprise not even be on the roadmap.

Do you know how much additional work it actually takes to pull these out manually.

Monday lacks where the big boys like SalesForce clean up. I love Monday, but only ever for our SMB clients as monday.com still lacks the functionality required to play at the enterprise level.

posts.author: TMcKeel (Tim McKeel)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, five years later and this is still an issue. I exported the entire block of ‘Account data’ and this information was not there. Not only do I need to be able to export the automations for backup purposes, but need the ability to export the automation history. I can see the history, but have to manually expand each entry to see full details. Would be nice to export and use filters.

posts.author: tekMike (Mike hayles)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree, how the heck is this still not in play!!!

We have boards with 90 automations and connected through Make.com to be able to connect multiple boards above the standard 20 in play. It’s crazy as most project managers are in the game of documenting everything and this MP TOOL can’t do that!!!

posts.author: Kshaw01 (Kasandra Shaw)
posts.cookedHtml: Certified Monday administrator at a very large company here. This feature needs to be addressed ASAP. It is desperately needed by the systems engineers deisgning and maintaining these monday boards and their automations.

posts.author: Jonathan-BookCheck (Jonathan Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml: Bump for interest. Would be great if it can be bundled (as a separate tab) with the existing option to export boards (with/without updates and subitems) to Excel.
